topic: An athlete doesn't make the team they expected to join
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp autumn air felt like a physical weight on Leo’s shoulders as he stood by the gymnasium doors. He clutched the worn strap of his duffel bag, the leather digging into his palm. The cheers of the other hopefuls, the easy camaraderie of the incoming squad, washed over him like a tidal wave, leaving him stranded on a distant, dry shore. He watched them walk in, their laughter echoing off the polished floor, and a tight knot formed in his chest. He tried to force a casual smile, but his lips felt stiff, refusing to curve upward. When the coach finally called the names, his heart hammered against his ribs, a frantic, uneven rhythm. When his name wasn't called, the silence that followed felt deafening, amplifying the slight tremor in his hands. He managed a nod, a small, jerky movement, and turned to leave, acutely aware of every eye on him.

[story 2]

“Well, that was a performance, Leo,” Coach Miller said, his tone measured, devoid of any fanfare. The words landed in the room with the precision of a thrown dart. Leo swallowed hard, the dryness in his throat making the motion difficult. He could feel the heat rising up his neck, a flush spreading across his cheeks that he desperately wished he could extinguish. He kept his gaze fixed on the scuffed toes of his sneakers, focusing intently on the pattern in the linoleum. Every casual glance from a teammate felt like a spotlight, illuminating the stark reality of his exclusion. When the final roster was posted, his eyes scanned the list, and when he realized his name wasn't there, a strange hollowness settled in his stomach, a cold, heavy sensation that made him want to shrink into the shadows.

[story 3]

"Next up!" The announcer’s voice boomed, and Leo took a deep, shaky breath, steeling himself for the inevitable. He walked onto the field, the bright stadium lights seeming to mock his solitude. He could hear the murmur of approval from the assembled group, a sound that seemed miles away. When the coach began reading the names, Leo’s focus narrowed to a painful point. Each name that was called felt like a tiny brick being placed on a wall separating him from the group. When the announcement concluded and the collective energy shifted to the next selection, Leo felt a sudden, overwhelming urge to simply disappear. He shuffled his feet, the movement clumsy and exaggerated, acutely aware of the awkward stillness he projected amidst the vibrant energy of the team.
